title: Unit, Integration, CLI, Fixture, and Changed-Case Testing · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss decisions for unit, integration, cli, fixture, and changed-case testing and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit limitations, and named browser, filesystem, terminal, packaging, operating-system, load, security, and release transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for risk-layered Bookbot test suite. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evi…
objectives: Partition tests across pure units, file integration, CLI behavior, report snapshots, package installation, and stakeholder acceptance.
Use named table and subtest cases for empty, punctuation, Unicode, malformed, large, tied, and boundary inputs.
Use real temporary files and directories for filesystem contracts and reserve mocks for narrow failure injection.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

For diagnose the hidden failure, run one convenient ASCII text, omit corpus authority, repository and policy identity, bytes, encoding, Unicode, offsets, reconciliation, ties, stderr, status, changed cases, tests, recovery, and native transfer, then accept a plausible report as Bookbot proof. This does not satisfy Test Quality for risk-layered Bookbot test suite.

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Test Quality
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Test Quality: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Bookbot team handles case 1588 during a multilingual community archive intake. Build deterministic Python evidence to layer unit, real-file integration, CLI, changed-defect, and installed smoke evidence; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of host file, process, Git, package-install, credential, and release effects; then introduce an equal-frequency tie and prove the secondary ranking key is stable. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Prove tests fail under a deliberate changed behavior and protect against order, locale, encoding, platform, and stale-fixture coupling.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Test Quality.
steps.why: Retrieving Test Quality through the concrete risk-layered Bookbot test suite case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Bookbot and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Test Quality, keep Test Quality connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Prove tests fail under a deliberate changed behavior and protect against order, locale, encoding, platform, and stale-fixture coupling.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Test Quality, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Test Quality precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Test Quality, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A quiz Bookbot team handles case 1588 during a multilingual community archive intake. Build deterministic Python evidence to layer unit, real-file integration, CLI, changed-defect, and installed smoke evidence; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of host file, process, Git, package-install, credential, and release effects; then introduce an equal-frequency tie and prove the secondary ranking key is stable. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Prove tests fail under a deliberate changed behavior and protect against order, locale, encoding, platform, and stale-fixture coupling.

For Bookbot and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Test Quality, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Code coverage percentage establishes that assertions detect defects.
